topic: {{if}} conditional rendering
instruction: How do I conditionally show content in a GJS template using {{if}}?
response: Use the `{{if}}` helper to conditionally render elements in the template. ```gjs ``` `{{if}}` takes a condition as the first argument and renders the second argument only when the condition is truthy. The falsy case (empty string, null, undefined, false, 0) causes nothing to render. Use it for simple conditional visibility.

topic: {{unless}} conditional rendering
instruction: How does {{unless}} work in a GJS template? Show an example.
response: `{{unless}}` is the inverse of `{{if}}` — it renders content when the condition is falsy. ```gjs ``` `{{unless @isOut}}` renders the badge only when `@isOut` is falsy (player is still at the crease). It reads more naturally than `{{if (not @isOut)}}`. Use `{{unless}}` when you want to show something in the "normal" case and hide it in the "exception" case.

topic: {{if}} with {{else}}
instruction: How do I use {{if}} with an {{else}} block in GJS to show different content?
response: Use the block form of `{{if}}` with `{{else}}` to render one of two alternatives. ```gjs ``` The block form uses `{{#if}}` ... `{{else}}` ... `{{/if}}`. This is ideal for loading/loaded states or showing different UIs for true/false conditions.

topic: {{each-in}} for objects
instruction: How do I iterate over the keys and values of a plain object in a GJS template?
response:
Use `{{#each-in}}` to iterate over an object's enumerable properties.

```gjs
<template>
  <dl class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2 p-4 bg-gray-50 rounded">
    {{#each-in @matchStats as |key value|}}
      <dt class="text-sm text-gray-500 font-medium capitalize">{{key}}</dt>
      <dd class="text-sm font-bold text-gray-900 text-right">{{value}}</dd>
    {{/each-in}}
  </dl>
</template>
```

`{{#each-in @matchStats as |key value|}}` gives you both the property name and its value per iteration. This is useful for displaying arbitrary key-value data like match statistics without knowing all keys upfront.

topic: {{yield}} for block content
instruction: How do I make a GJS component accept block content using {{yield}}?
response: Add `{{yield}}` inside your component's template where you want block content to appear. ```gjs import Component from '@glimmer/component'; export default class Card extends Component { } ``` Then use it as a block component from the parent: ```gjs import Card from './card'; import PlayerStats from './player-stats'; ``` `{{yield}}` is the slot where block content is rendered. The parent decides what goes inside, making the `Card` component reusable for any content.

topic: named blocks with {{yield to='name'}}
instruction: How do I use named blocks in GJS to yield content into specific slots?
response: Use `{{yield to="slotName"}}` in the component and `<:slotName>` in the parent to fill named slots. ```gjs import Component from '@glimmer/component'; export default class MatchPanel extends Component { } ``` Parent usage: ```gjs import MatchPanel from './match-panel'; ``` Named blocks give consumers precise control over each region of a component's layout.

topic: ...attributes splattributes
instruction: What is ...attributes in GJS and how do I use it?
response:
`...attributes` forwards any HTML attributes or modifiers passed by the parent onto a DOM element.

```gjs
import Component from '@glimmer/component';

export default class BaseButton extends Component {
  <template>
    <button
      type="button"
      class="px-4 py-2 rounded font-medium transition-colors {{@variant}}"
      ...attributes
    >
      {{yield}}
    </button>
  </template>
}
```

Parent usage:

```gjs
import BaseButton from './base-button';

<template>
  <BaseButton
    @variant="bg-green-600 text-white hover:bg-green-700"
    disabled={{@isSubmitting}}
    data-testid="submit-btn"
    {{on "click" @onSubmit}}
  >
    Submit Score
  </BaseButton>
</template>
```

`...attributes` lets the parent set `class`, `id`, `data-*`, `aria-*`, event modifiers, and any HTML attribute without the component needing to explicitly forward each one.

topic: using {{array}} helper
instruction: How do I create an inline array in a GJS template using the {{array}} helper?
response:
Use the `{{array}}` helper to create a temporary array directly in the template without needing a getter.

```gjs
import { array } from '@ember/component/helper';

<template>
  <nav class="flex gap-2 p-2 bg-gray-100 rounded">
    {{#each (array "All" "Live" "Upcoming" "Completed") as |tab|}}
      <button
        type="button"
        class="px-3 py-1.5 text-sm rounded-md hover:bg-white hover:shadow transition-all"
      >
        {{tab}}
      </button>
    {{/each}}
  </nav>
</template>
```

`(array "a" "b" "c")` creates an array inline in the template. It's useful for static lists of options, tabs, or any enumerable set that doesn't need to come from component state. Note the parenthesis form `(array ...)` for use as a sub-expression.

topic: how component scope works in GJS
instruction: How does JavaScript scope work in a GJS component? What is accessible in the template?
response: In GJS, the template has access to the same scope as the surrounding JavaScript module — this is what makes GJS powerful. **Template scope includes:** 1. **Module-level imports** — Any `import` at the top of the file 2. **Module-level variables/functions** — Constants and helper functions defined outside the class 3. **Class instance** — Via `this` for properties, getters, and actions 4. **Component args** — Via `@argName` ```gjs import Component from '@glimmer/component'; import { formatRuns } from '../helpers/format'; // ✅ module import — in scope import ScoreBadge from './score-badge'; // ✅ module import — in scope const MAX_OVERS = 20; // ✅ module constant — in scope const statusLabel = (isOut) => isOut ? 'OUT' : 'BAT'; // ✅ module function export default class BatsmanRow extends Component { get formattedRuns() { return formatRuns(this.args.runs); // ✅ class method } } ``` This is fundamentally different from .hbs files where only globally registered items and `this` were accessible.
